name: Anthropic API
what_it_does: Anthropic Claude REST API — safety-focused frontier AI platform enabling agents to invoke Claude models for text generation, vision, tool use, extended thinking, computer use, and document analysis with industry-leading instruction following and long-context reasoning.
use_cases: Agents using Claude for multi-step tool use — Claude's tool_use content blocks support parallel and sequential tool calls with clear stop_reason signals for agentic loop control
Extended thinking for complex reasoning — agents enabling Claude's extended thinking mode to solve hard analytical, mathematical, or strategic problems with transparent step-by-step reasoning
Document analysis — agents sending PDF files directly via the Files API or base64-encoded documents for Claude to extract, summarize, or answer questions about long-form content
Computer use agent — agents using Claude's computer_use tool to control desktop applications, browsers, and GUIs via screenshot → action → screenshot loops
Long-context document processing — agents leveraging Claude's 200K token context window to process entire codebases, legal documents, or research papers in a single API call
not_for: Multimodal image generation — Claude is a text and vision model; it cannot generate images; use DALL-E, Stable Diffusion, or Midjourney for image generation
Real-time speech-to-speech — Claude processes text and images; for voice AI agents use Vapi, Retell, or OpenAI Realtime API
Fastest possible inference — Claude prioritizes quality over raw speed; use Groq or Fireworks AI when tokens-per-second is the primary constraint
best_when: You need an AI model that excels at following complex instructions, long-context analysis (up to 200K tokens), nuanced reasoning, and agentic tool use with predictable behavior — particularly for enterprise and safety-critical applications.
avoid_when: You need image generation, voice/audio capabilities, maximum inference speed, or a model available as a managed service on GCP/Azure (use Vertex AI or Azure for Claude in those clouds).

auth.notes: API key as x-api-key header. anthropic-version header required (current: 2023-06-01). Workspaces for organizational key management. No OAuth. Keys scoped to workspace. Usage tracked per key with configurable spend limits. Anthropic-Beta header for beta features (extended thinking, computer use).

pricing.paid_tiers: Claude 3.5 Sonnet: $3/$15 per 1M input/output tokens; Claude 3.5 Haiku: $0.80/$4 per 1M tokens; Claude 3 Opus: $15/$75 per 1M tokens; prompt caching at 90% discount for cached tokens
pricing.notes: Prompt caching (cache_control) dramatically reduces costs for repeated context — system prompts and large documents cached at 90% discount after first call. Batch API (Message Batches) at 50% discount for async workloads. No free tier beyond initial API credits.

performance.rate_limits: Tier-based by spend: Build tier (default): 50 RPM, 50K TPM; higher tiers based on usage history; custom rate limits for enterprise

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent documentation with dedicated agents guide, tool use cookbook, and error reference. Errors include error type and message. Overload errors (529) require exponential backoff. Beta feature errors clearly indicate missing Anthropic-Beta header.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Messages API is stateless. Same input with temperature=0 produces highly consistent but not guaranteed identical outputs. Message Batches have batch IDs for idempotent status polling. No client-side idempotency keys for individual messages.
known_agent_gotchas: Tool use requires checking stop_reason == 'tool_use' AND iterating over all content blocks for tool_use type — agents that only check stop_reason miss multi-tool responses
Extended thinking tokens count toward both input and output token billing — very long thinking budgets can dramatically increase costs; set max_tokens_to_sample appropriately
Computer use requires Anthropic-Beta: computer-use-2024-10-22 header — missing beta header returns 400 error with no mention of the required header
Prompt caching requires explicit cache_control: {type: ephemeral} markers on content blocks — caching is opt-in and does not happen automatically; without it, repeated context is billed at full price
Rate limits reset per minute — agents hitting 529 Overloaded (separate from 429 rate limit) indicate server capacity issues requiring longer backoff than standard rate limit errors

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001 certified. HIPAA BAA available. GDPR compliant. TLS enforced. Data not used for model training without explicit consent. Workspace-level key management. No granular API key scopes. Constitutional AI safety approach built into models.
